questions.question_text: In the Indian system, which is the correct way to place commas in 84572619?
questions.explanation: In the Indian system, the last three digits stay together first, and then commas are placed after every two digits moving left. So 84572619 becomes 8,45,72,619.

questions.question_text: How is 12,304,508 read in the International system?
questions.explanation: In the International system, digits are grouped in threes as millions, thousands, and ones. So 12,304,508 is read as twelve million three hundred four thousand five hundred eight.

options.option_text: Twelve lakh thirty-four thousand five hundred eight
One crore twenty-three lakh four thousand five hundred eight
Twelve million three hundred four thousand five hundred eight
Twelve crore three lakh four thousand five hundred eight

questions.question_text: Which number in the International system is equal to 1,25,00,000 in the Indian system?
questions.explanation: 1,25,00,000 means 1 crore 25 lakh, which is equal to 12,500,000 in the International system.

questions.question_text: Which number is written correctly in the International system?
questions.explanation: In the International system, commas are placed after every three digits from the right. So 65,432,189 follows the rule correctly.

questions.question_text: Which label belongs to the Indian numeration system?
questions.explanation: The Indian system uses terms like lakh and crore. The International system uses thousand, million, and billion instead.

questions.question_text: How many lakhs make one crore?
questions.explanation: One crore equals 1,00,00,000, and one lakh equals 1,00,000. So 100 lakhs make one crore.

questions.question_text: Which is the correct reading of 3,05,00,018 in the Indian system?
questions.explanation: The groups are 3 crore, 5 lakh, 0 thousand, and 18. So the correct reading is three crore five lakh eighteen.

questions.question_text: Which Indian-system number equals 7,500,000 in the International system?
questions.explanation: 7,500,000 is written with Indian comma grouping as 75,00,000. That is seventy-five lakh.

questions.question_text: Which notation follows the Indian grouping rule correctly?
questions.explanation: In the Indian system, after the last three digits, commas come after every two digits. 4,23,15,609 follows that pattern correctly.

questions.question_text: The word “million” belongs to the International system of numeration.
questions.explanation: Million is a place-value term used in the International system. The Indian system uses lakh and crore instead.

questions.question_text: Which numeral matches “thirty-two million forty thousand nineteen”?
questions.explanation: Thirty-two million is 32,000,000, forty thousand is 40,000, and nineteen is 19. So the numeral is 32,040,019.

questions.question_text: Which statement is correct?
questions.explanation: 4,09,75,210 in the Indian system equals 40,975,210 in the International system. That is greater than 40,875,210.

questions.question_text: A website shows a reach of “twenty-three million six hundred thousand forty.” Which numeral matches it?
questions.explanation: Twenty-three million is 23,000,000, six hundred thousand is 600,000, and forty is 40. Together they make 23,600,040.

questions.question_text: In the number 92,407,315, what is the value of the digit 4?
questions.explanation: In 92,407,315, the digit 4 is in the hundred-thousands place. So its value is 400,000.

questions.question_text: Which is the correct expanded form of 4,08,05,302?
questions.explanation: The number has 4 crore, 8 lakh, 5 thousand, 3 hundred, and 2 ones. So the correct expanded form is 4,00,00,000 + 8,00,000 + 5,000 + 300 + 2.

questions.question_text: Which list is arranged from smallest to largest?
questions.explanation: Convert all to one system before comparing. 5,07,09,210 = 50,709,210, 50,790,210 stays the same, and 5,08,09,210 = 50,809,210. So the correct order is 5,07,09,210, 50,790,210, 5,08,09,210.

questions.question_text: A student says, “If the number of digits is the same, comma placement must also be the same in every numeration system.” Which correction is best?
questions.explanation: The same digits can be grouped differently in different numeration systems. The Indian system uses a 3,2,2 pattern, while the International system uses a 3,3,3 pattern.

options.option_text: The student is correct because commas never depend on the system
The student is correct only for seven-digit numbers
The student is incorrect because the two systems group digits differently
The student is incorrect because commas are used only in the Indian system

questions.question_text: A student wrote the number name of 7,08,04,015 as “seven crore eight lakh four thousand fifteen.” What is the mistake?
questions.explanation: The grouping 7 crore, 8 lakh, 4 thousand, and 15 is correct. So there is no mistake in the reading.

options.option_text: The student should have said “seven crore eighty lakh...”
The student should have added “and fifteen hundred”
The student should have said “seven crore eight lakh four lakh fifteen”

questions.question_text: Write “nine crore two lakh forty thousand six” in figures using the Indian system.
questions.explanation: Nine crore is 9,00,00,000, two lakh is 2,00,000, forty thousand is 40,000, and six is 6. Together they make 9,02,40,006.

questions.question_text: Which of the following are written in the International system correctly? Select all that apply.
questions.explanation: In the International system, commas are placed after every three digits from the right. So 18,507,250 and 4,300,019 are correct, while 1,85,07,250 and 43,00,019 are not.

questions.question_text: 54,321,008 and 5,43,21,008 represent the same quantity.
questions.explanation: The digits are the same; only the comma placement differs according to the system used. So both represent the same quantity.

questions.question_text: A report says a city received 1,85,07,250 tourists. Which International-system form shows the same number?
questions.explanation: 1,85,07,250 in the Indian system equals 18,507,250 in the International system. Only the comma grouping changes.

questions.question_text: Sana converted 23,456,019 into the Indian system as 2,34,56,019. Is she correct?
questions.explanation: In the Indian system, 23,456,019 is grouped as 2,34,56,019. So Sana's conversion is correct.

options.option_text: Yes, because the commas now follow the Indian 3,2,2 pattern
No, because the number should stay unchanged
No, because Indian notation does not use commas

questions.question_text: Write 34,508,612 in the Indian system.
questions.explanation: Keep the digits the same and change only the comma grouping. In the Indian system, 34,508,612 becomes 3,45,08,612.

questions.question_text: A chart shows these visitor counts: 12,304,508, 11,999,999, 12,040,890, and 12,300,450. Which is the greatest?
questions.explanation: Compare the millions first. All except 11,999,999 have 12 million. Between the 12-million values, 12,304,508 is greater than 12,300,450 and 12,040,890.

questions.question_text: Which number name matches 4,50,00,005 in the Indian system?
questions.explanation: 4,50,00,005 means 4 crore 50 lakh 0 thousand 5. So it is read as four crore fifty lakh five.

questions.question_text: Which numeral matches “seventy-one million two hundred thousand sixty”?
questions.explanation: Seventy-one million is 71,000,000, two hundred thousand is 200,000, and sixty is 60. So the numeral is 71,200,060.

questions.question_text: Which pair represents the same number?
questions.explanation: 2,30,40,500 in the Indian system equals 23,040,500 in the International system. The digits stay the same but are regrouped.

questions.question_text: In 5,43,21,008, what is the value of the digit 4?
questions.explanation: In 5,43,21,008, the digit 4 is in the ten-lakh place inside 43 lakh. So its value is 40,00,000.

questions.question_text: Which expanded form is correct for 12,304,508?
questions.explanation: 12,304,508 means 10,000,000 + 2,000,000 + 300,000 + 4,000 + 500 + 8. That follows the International place values correctly.

questions.question_text: Write 18,507,250 in the Indian system.
questions.explanation: Only the comma grouping changes. In the Indian system, 18,507,250 is written as 1,85,07,250.

questions.question_text: A table lists these numbers in different systems: 3,45,08,612, 34,508,611, 3,45,80,612, and 34,580,612. Which is greatest?
questions.explanation: Convert mentally if needed. 3,45,80,612 equals 34,580,612, which is greater than 34,508,612, 34,508,611, and 34,580,612 ties with itself if written differently. So 3,45,80,612 is the greatest form shown.

questions.question_text: Aman says 7,023,415 becomes 70,23,415 in the Indian system. What is the correct correction?
questions.explanation: The last three digits stay together first. Then the remaining digits are grouped in twos. So 7,023,415 becomes 70,23,415. Aman is correct.

options.option_text: No correction is needed because 70,23,415 is correct

questions.question_text: Rita wrote 53,412,76 as a valid International-system number. What is wrong?
questions.explanation: In the International system, digits must be grouped in threes from the right. 53,412,76 breaks that rule because the comma grouping is inconsistent.

options.option_text: It should have no commas at all
Its commas do not follow the 3-digit grouping rule
It should be read in the Indian system only

questions.explanation: 1,25,00,000 means 1 crore 25 lakh. That is 10,000,000 + 2,500,000, which totals 12,500,000. The value is the same; only the grouping changes.

options.option_text: Because crore and million always have the same value
Because both notations represent the same quantity with different grouping styles

questions.question_text: Which Indian-system form should be used for 90,400,050?
questions.explanation: Keep the digits the same and regroup from the right using 3,2,2 commas. 90,400,050 becomes 9,04,00,050.

questions.question_text: A scholarship notice lists the total fund as 12,75,40,000. Which interpretation is correct?
questions.explanation: 12,75,40,000 is grouped as 12 crore, 75 lakh, and 40 thousand. So it is read as twelve crore seventy-five lakh forty thousand.

options.option_text: One hundred twenty-seven million four hundred thousand
Twelve crore seventy-five lakh forty thousand
One crore twenty-seven lakh fifty-four thousand

questions.question_text: Why is 2,501,08,340 difficult to accept as a standard representation?
questions.explanation: It mixes grouping styles instead of following a single system properly. A valid number should follow either the Indian grouping rule or the International grouping rule consistently.

options.option_text: Because it mixes grouping styles instead of following one system consistently
Because it has more than seven digits
Because commas are used only in the Indian system
Because zero cannot appear after a comma

questions.question_text: Which statements about 54,321,008 are true? Select all that apply.
questions.explanation: 54,321,008 is in the International system and can be written in Indian form as 5,43,21,008. It is more than fifty million and less than sixty million. But it is not equal to 5,43,210.

options.option_text: In the Indian system, it is written as 5,43,21,008

questions.question_text: Convert 6,75,04,200 into the International system.
questions.explanation: 6,75,04,200 means 6 crore 75 lakh 4 thousand 200. In the International system, it is written as 67,504,200.

questions.question_text: Write 107,540,019 in the Indian system.
questions.explanation: Regroup 107,540,019 using Indian commas. The correct Indian-system form is 10,75,40,019.

questions.question_text: Which of the following cannot be a correct Indian-system representation?
questions.explanation: The Indian system uses one group of three digits at the end and then groups of two digits. 9,876,543 follows International grouping, so it is not a correct Indian-system representation.

questions.question_text: Four stadium counts are shown: 1,85,07,250, 18,570,249, 1,84,99,999, and 18,507,251. Which is the highest attendance?
In 6,43,08,512, what is the place value of the digit 3?
questions.explanation: 6,43,08,512 is read as 6 crore 43 lakh 8 thousand 512. The digit 3 is in the lakh place, so its value is 3,00,000.

questions.question_text: A student converts 45,006,120 into the Indian system as 4,50,06,120. What should be fixed?
questions.explanation: 45,006,120 should be grouped as 4,50,06,120 in the Indian system. So the conversion is already correct.

questions.question_text: Which number name matches 70,023,415 in the International system?
questions.explanation: 70,023,415 is 70 million 23 thousand 415. So the correct reading is seventy million twenty-three thousand four hundred fifteen.

options.option_text: Seven million twenty-three thousand four hundred fifteen
Seventy crore twenty-three thousand four hundred fifteen
Seventy million twenty-three thousand four hundred fifteen
Seven crore two lakh thirty-four thousand fifteen

questions.question_text: 50,000,000 and 5,00,00,000 represent the same quantity.
questions.explanation: 50,000,000 in International grouping is 5 crore in Indian grouping, written as 5,00,00,000. So the statement is true.

questions.question_text: Convert 2,50,40,000 into the International system and write its number name.
questions.explanation: 2,50,40,000 equals 25,040,000 in the International system. Its number name is twenty-five million forty thousand.

questions.question_text: Which conclusions about 3,05,00,018 are correct? Select all that apply.
questions.explanation: 3,05,00,018 is read as three crore five lakh eighteen. In the International system, it is 30,500,018. It is greater than three crore, and it does not contain any thousands part.

options.option_text: In the International system, it is 30,500,018
It has a non-zero thousands part
It is read as three crore five lakh eighteen

questions.explanation: Both notations use the same digits in the same order. The only difference is comma placement based on the system used. So the quantity represented is the same.

options.option_text: Because the Indian system makes numbers smaller
Because the International system removes one digit
Because only the grouping style changes, not the value of the digits
Because lakh and thousand always mean the same thing
